body.title: LegacyDirectClaimError: cmd_claim raises when called without cmd_next context
body.result: LegacyDirectClaimError env-flag arm in adopt_next_ticket + 3 tests (strict-claim, channel-post, env-flag-before-chain); fixed TestLaunchNextWorkerDispatch Cortex isolation as incidental
body.description:
Old Igor habits describe the direct-claim model: 'claim a ticket, work it.' After adopt_next_ticket migrates to cmd_next, any remaining direct-claim code should surface immediately in logs rather than silently misfiring.

Add LegacyDirectClaimError to cc_queue.py cmd_claim: when the env var IGOR_STRICT_CLAIM_MODEL=1 is set (worker_foreman.py sets this before pe_chain runs), any cmd_claim call raises with: 'Direct ticket claiming is deprecated — use cmd_next --max-difficulty=N to request tickets.' Log the error to the channel so it surfaces in CC inbox. CC-side cmd_claim (no env var) is unaffected.

**Affected files:** lab/claudecode/cc_queue.py (LegacyDirectClaimError class + check in cmd_claim), wild_igor/igor/tools/worker_foreman.py (set IGOR_STRICT_CLAIM_MODEL=1 env before pe_chain call)
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; error must post to channel (cc_queue already has channel post patterns)
**Scope boundary:** IN: LegacyDirectClaimError class; env-flag check in cmd_claim; env var set in adopt_next_ticket. OUT: pe_claim behavior when env flag not set; CC-side claiming
**Test plan:** cmd_claim with IGOR_STRICT_CLAIM_MODEL=1 raises LegacyDirectClaimError; cmd_claim without the flag works normally; adopt_next_ticket integration: env var is set before pe_chain subprocess
